title: Scrap Rate Optimizer
description: Industrial-grade tool for calculating and optimizing scrap rates in manufacturing processes, aligned with Lean, Six Sigma, and ISO 9001 standards.

inputs.label: Total Units Produced
inputs.businessContext: Total number of units produced in the reporting period (e.g., shift, day, week).

businessContext_i18n.en: Total number of units produced in the reporting period (e.g., shift, day, week).

inputs.label: Defective Units
inputs.businessContext: Number of units that failed quality inspection or are non-conforming.

businessContext_i18n.en: Number of units that failed quality inspection or are non-conforming.

inputs.label: Rework Units
inputs.businessContext: Units that were reworked to meet specifications (counted separately from scrap).

businessContext_i18n.en: Units that were reworked to meet specifications (counted separately from scrap).

inputs.label: Material Cost per Unit
inputs.businessContext: Direct material cost per unit in USD.

businessContext_i18n.en: Direct material cost per unit in USD.

inputs.label: Labor Cost per Unit
inputs.businessContext: Direct labor cost per unit in USD.

businessContext_i18n.en: Direct labor cost per unit in USD.

inputs.label: Overhead Cost per Unit
inputs.businessContext: Allocated overhead cost per unit in USD.

businessContext_i18n.en: Allocated overhead cost per unit in USD.

inputs.label: Process Stage
inputs.businessContext: Stage in the production process where scrap is measured.

businessContext_i18n.en: Stage in the production process where scrap is measured.

inputs.label: Include Rework in Scrap Calculation
inputs.businessContext: If true, rework units are treated as scrap for cost calculations (conservative approach).

businessContext_i18n.en: If true, rework units are treated as scrap for cost calculations (conservative approach).

rules.message: Defective units cannot exceed total units produced.
Rework units cannot exceed total units produced.
All cost inputs must be positive.

thresholds.message: Scrap rate exceeds 10% - immediate process review required.
Scrap rate between 5% and 10% - investigate root causes.
Total scrap cost exceeds $50,000 - escalate to management.

formulas.name: Scrap Rate
formulas.description: Primary scrap rate as a fraction of total production.

formulas.name: Rework Rate
formulas.description: Rework units as a fraction of total production.

formulas.name: Unit Cost
formulas.description: Total cost per unit (material + labor + overhead).

formulas.name: Total Scrap Cost
formulas.description: Total cost of scrap units (including rework if selected).

formulas.name: Scrap Cost as Percentage of Total Production Cost
formulas.description: Scrap cost relative to total production cost.

formulas.name: First Pass Yield (FPY)
formulas.description: Percentage of units produced without any defect or rework.

primary.label: Scrap Rate
primary.description: Primary scrap rate (defective units / total units).

scrap_cost_percentage.label: Scrap Cost % of Total Production Cost

hiddenLossDrivers.label: High Defect Rate in Machining Stage
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Scrap rate in machining stage is 3x higher than assembly stage.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Rework Cost Amplification
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Rework units consume additional labor and overhead, increasing effective cost per unit by 40%.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Material Waste from Dimensional Defects
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Dimensional defects account for 60% of all scrap material cost.

suggestedActions.description: Implement SPC (Statistical Process Control) on machining parameters to reduce dimensional defects.
Conduct root cause analysis on rework drivers; consider design for manufacturability (DFM) changes.
Train operators on visual inspection criteria to catch defects earlier in the process.
